comments.content:
This is the central UI pattern used by **10 menu screens**. It creates a grid of difficulty-graded buttons organized into named columns. Key mechanics:

1. **Responsive button width** (lines 42-58): Auto-calculates based on screen/window width, 28% per column, capped at 160-340px
2. **Difficulty color gradient** (lines 22-32): Buttons are color-coded from beginner→elite based on position index
3. **Advanced toggle** (lines 130-152): Items beyond 60% of the longest column are hidden behind a "SHOW ADVANCED" toggle
4. **Grid layout**: Column headers in row 0, buttons in subsequent rows, centered alignment

**Problem**: Every preset is a hardcoded button with a lambda that calls `navigator.launch_exercise()` with fixed params. No way to tweak individual parameters — users must pick from a finite list of presets.
comments.author: agent

comments.content: Convenience wrapper that splits a flat list of presets into two columns (first half → "FIXED / BASIC", second half → "MIXED / ADVANCED"). Used only by **FlashMenuScreen** and **WordsMenuScreen**. Delegates entirely to `_create_column_menu`.
comments.author: agent

comments.content:
This is the **only menu screen** that uses a custom two-panel layout instead of `_create_column_menu`. It has:
- **Left panel (2/3 width)**: Multi-select category toggle buttons with Select All/Clear
- **Right panel (1/3 width)**: Display time (radio-style single-select), slides per session, lines per slide, and START button

This is the closest existing pattern to the desired unified two-panel config layout. It maintains state across `build()` calls via instance variables (`_selected_categories`, `_selected_time`, etc.).
comments.author: agent

comments.content:
**No menu screen** — launched directly via `nav.launch_exercise(SchulteExercise)` from dashboard. The `start()` method renders its own config screen with:
- Grid size slider (3–7)
- Cell size slider (Small/Medium/Large/XL)
- START button + Ctrl+Enter shortcut

Persists defaults via `theme_manager.schulte_grid_size` / `schulte_cell_idx`.
comments.author: agent

comments.content:
**No menu screen** — launched directly via `nav.launch_exercise(PacerExercise)`. The `start()` method renders its own config screen with:
- Text input area (QTextEdit, 15 lines, 60% screen width)
- WPM slider
- Mode selector (radio buttons: single/multi/z-pattern/peripheral/speed-burst)
- Words/chunk slider
- Lines/group slider (conditional on mode)
- START button + Ctrl+Enter shortcut
comments.author: agent

comments.content:
**No menu screen** — launched directly via `nav.launch_exercise(SpacedRepetitionExercise)`. The `start()` method renders a **deck selection screen** with:
- Two-column layout: deck list (2/3) | options (1/3)
- Scrollable deck cards with stats (due count, mastered count)
- Review/Study/Read buttons per deck
- Create Custom Deck button

This is a **unique pattern** — more of a deck manager than a config screen.
comments.author: agent

comments.content:
**Has BOTH** a column menu screen (`MotMenuScreen`) AND an inline config screen. The menu provides 12 presets across 3 difficulty columns. The inline config (shown when exercise starts) has:
- Targets slider (2–8)
- Distractors slider (3–15)
- Speed slider (1–10)
- Duration slider (3–15s)
- Rounds slider (3–10)
- START button + Ctrl+Enter

Menu presets pre-populate the sliders via kwargs, but user can still adjust before starting.
comments.author: agent

comments.content:
**Has BOTH** a column menu screen (`PeripheralFlashMenuScreen`) AND an inline config screen. The menu provides 27 presets across 3 columns (Letters/Numbers/Shapes). The inline config has:
- Stimulus type combo (Letters/Numbers/Shapes)
- Flash duration slider (ms)
- Eccentricity slider (%)
- Rounds slider
- START button + Ctrl+Enter
comments.author: agent

- Grid size slider (3–7)
- Cell size slider (Small/Medium/Large/XL)
- Time limit slider (0=unlimited, 15–90s)
- START button + Ctrl+Enter

comments.content: **No inline config** — `start()` requires explicit parameters (`mode`, `rounds`, `level_func`, `span_config`). The exercise immediately begins the first round. All configuration comes from the 3 menu screens: `FlashMenuScreen`, `WordsMenuScreen`, `EyespanMenuScreen`.
comments.author: agent

comments.content: **No inline config** — `start()` accepts explicit parameters (`mode`, `delay`, `duration_s`, `cycles`) and immediately begins the exercise. All configuration comes from `PrimingMenuScreen`.
comments.author: agent

comments.content: **No inline config** — `start()` accepts explicit parameters (`mode`, `start_length`, `flash_ms`, `rounds`) and immediately begins. All configuration comes from `SequenceMemoryMenuScreen`.
comments.author: agent

comments.content:
**Has BOTH** a custom two-panel menu screen (`SlideProcessingMenuScreen`) AND an inline config screen. Unique behavior: when launched from the menu with comma-separated categories (line 70), it **skips** the inline config and goes straight to the exercise. When launched directly (e.g., from a training path), it shows the inline config with:
- Category combo
- Display time slider
- Slides per session slider
- Lines per slide slider
- START button + Ctrl+Enter
comments.author: agent
